summary: Decompiled/refactored much of lbRefract by replacing opaque state and M2C-style accesses with typed HSD/GX code, adding refraction texture generation/init, DObj/PObj class hooks, GX indirect-texture render setup, and callback-based texture read/write helpers. It also fixed THPDec_80331340's prototype/call sites to pass width. The bot reported +1848 matched code bytes and 9 new matches, with a lbrefract .sdata regression.
key_files: src/melee/lb/lbrefract.c (+535/-51): major lbrefract decomp; typed globals/data layout; refraction buffer generation/init; DObj/PObj hooks; GX indirect TEV setup; texture callbacks
src/melee/lb/lbrefract.h (+1/-1): changed lbRefract_80022998 third parameter from s8 to s32
src/melee/lb/lbmthp.c (+3/-3): passed width to THPDec_80331340 in fn_8001EF5C and lbMthp8001FAA0
src/melee/lb/lbmthp.h (+1/-1): updated THPDec_80331340 prototype from four to five parameters
what_changed: Changed THPDec_80331340 from four args to five args in lbmthp.h; call sites now pass data->width or lbl_804335B8.x6C.
Added HSD/GX/lbArchive/OSCache-related includes and typed callback-helper prototypes in lbrefract.c, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for fn_80021F70, fn_80021FF8, and fn_8002206C.

Introduced lbRefract_DataLayout for lbl_803BB0B0 and lbl_804336D0_t for runtime state, with named fields such as refractionUserCount, image_ptr, and texture_mtx plus a size assert for the 0x40 state struct.
Implemented lbRefract_80021CE8, which generates refraction lookup texture data from archive-loaded parameters, writes through the callback table, and flushes the generated buffer.
Completed lbRefract_8002219C callback setup for numeric texture formats 3, 4, and 6, using s32 width/height and exact aligned row-stride calculations.
Added lbRefract_800222A4 initialization: load symbols, allocate the 0x140x0xF0 source texture, allocate per-entry TObj/image descriptor storage, generate 0x20x0x20 maps, and load TObj descriptors.

Rewrote lbRefract_8002247C to build texture_mtx from HSD_CObj projection_param fields for perspective, frustum, and ortho projection types; updated lbRefract_80022560 to copy into the typed image_ptr field.
Added HSD DObj/PObj class init functions fn_80022650 and fn_80022940, lbRefract_PObjLoad display-list patching for flag 0x2000, and lbRefract_80022998 GX indirect texture/TEV setup.
Updated refraction reference counting to use lbl_804336D0.refractionUserCount and replaced the old OSReport/__assert path with HSD_ASSERTREPORT at the same error site.
smart_moves: Typing lbl_804336D0 as a struct made later code readable and removed fragile int-array indexing while preserving size with STATIC_ASSERT.
Using real HSD_CObj projection fields replaced raw M2C_FIELD offset reads and helped lbRefract_8002247C reach a full match.
Keeping unknown helpers address-named but giving them precise signatures allowed callback-table behavior to be expressed without over-naming.
The texture callback split isolated format-specific tiled memory writes/reads while letting lbRefract_8002219C share stride and buffer-size setup.
The floating-point generator preserved decomp-sensitive details: __frsqrte Newton iterations, volatile f32 storage, explicit casts, and PAD_STACK.
The PObj loader derives display-list stride from GX vertex descriptors and flushes patched display data, matching the low-level GPU/cache behavior.
The THPDec_80331340 prototype fix was small but cross-file: updating the header and both call sites produced a new fn_8001EF5C match and improved lbMthp8001FAA0.
decomp_lessons: When HSD/baselib structs are known, prefer typed field access over M2C_FIELD offsets; it can improve both readability and matching.
Data-layout structs with offset comments and static size checks are useful for migrating labels into semantic code, but data sections can still regress and must be checked separately.
Header prototypes are part of matching work: one missing argument can block matches in unrelated call sites.
Use numeric formats or existing GX constants only when proven; this PR kept format dispatch on 3, 4, and 6 while adding GX headers for surrounding code.
Exact tiled texture address math and row alignment masks are important for GX texture-buffer helpers.
For math-heavy original code, reproduce the compiler's rsqrt/float-temp pattern rather than replacing it with cleaner library calls.
Display-list walkers need exact GXAttr/GXAttrType/component-size accounting and may require the original loop unrolling to approach a match.
naming_conventions: Public refraction functions kept the lbRefract_800xxxxx address-name style until semantic names are known.
Static unknown helpers remained fn_800xxxxx, but their prototypes were made concrete.
The runtime state struct used a label-derived name, lbl_804336D0_t, with understood fields named and unresolved fields left as unk_8 and unk_C.
The data-layout struct used a subsystem name, lbRefract_DataLayout, while preserving the original label lbl_803BB0B0.
Class library strings are explicit literals: refract_class_library, refract_dobj, and refract_pobj.
The public header changed lbRefract_80022998's third argument to s32, matching ABI-width use instead of the prior s8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(20) in lbRefract_80021CE8 to shape stack/codegen.
Used __frsqrte with repeated Newton refinement and a volatile f32 temp to match original sqrt-like behavior.
Preserved tiled texture offsets such as col >> 2, row masks, and 0xFFFFFFE0/0xFFFFFFC0 row-stride alignment.
Used exact GX state calls for indirect refraction: GXSetTexCoordGen2, GXLoadTexMtxImm, GXSetIndTexOrder, GXSetIndTexMtx, GXSetTevIndirect, and related TEV/Z/blend state.
Derived light projection matrices through MTXLightPerspective, MTXLightFrustum, and MTXLightOrtho with the observed 0.5F and -0.5F arguments.
In lbRefract_PObjLoad, used an 8-way unrolled display-list copy and DCFlushRange after patching.
Kept cache operations explicit: DCFlushRange for generated/patched refraction buffers and DCStoreRange in the THP decode path.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
PR body only said: Tracking PR as usual.
decomp-dev bot reported matched code 62.46% (+0.05%, +1848 bytes) and matched data 38.04% (-0.00%, -8 bytes).
Bot listed 9 new matches: lbRefract_80022998, fn_8002206C, fn_80021FF8, fn_80022650, fn_80022940, fn_80021F70, lbRefract_8002219C, lbRefract_8002247C, and fn_8001EF5C.
Bot also reported one broken match: main/melee/lb/lbrefract .sdata fell from 100.00% to 28.57%.
Partial improvements remained for lbRefract_80021CE8 at 98.12%, lbRefract_PObjLoad at 70.48%, and lbRefract_800222A4 at 84.44%.
follow_up_queries: Finish matching lbRefract_80021CE8, lbRefract_800222A4, and lbRefract_PObjLoad.
Investigate the bot-reported lbrefract .sdata regression and whether the new data-layout/global typing affected section ordering.
Resolve unknown names and types for lbl_804D63E8, unk_8, unk_C, fn_80021F70, fn_80021FF8, and fn_8002206C.
Replace numeric texture formats 3/4/6, flag 0x2000, and render-mode bits 0x20000000/0x08000000 with proven constants when evidence is available.
Check the HSD_ImageDesc storage typing around unk_8; the allocation size is count * 0x18 but the field is typed as HSD_ImageDesc** in this slice.
Trace the archive asset/symbol loaded through lbl_803BB0B0.filename and lbl_803BB0B0.symbol to name the refraction parameter table.
Name the fifth THPDec_80331340 argument once the decoder-side signature is better understood.
